title: 10 best herbal teas for acne
intro_desc: Herbal teas for acne are infusions made from various plants and herbs that are believed to help reduce acne symptoms. These teas are caffeine-free and rich in antioxidants, vitamins, and minerals that can improve skin health. Some popular herbal teas for acne include chamomile, green tea, and dandelion. Chamomile tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe irritated skin and reduce redness. Green tea is rich in antioxidants that can help reduce inflammation and prevent acne breakouts. Dandelion tea can help improve liver function, which can lead to clearer skin. By incorporating these herbal teas into their daily routine, individuals can improve their skin health, reduce acne symptoms, and boost their overall well-being.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow tea helps with acne because it has anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that can reduce redness, swelling, and the growth of bacteria that can cause acne. It contains flavonoids, alkaloids, and tannins that can help regulate sebum production, reducing the likelihood of clogged pores. Yarrow tea also has antioxidants that can protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, promoting skin health and reducing the appearance of acne. Additionally, yarrow tea can help soothe irritated skin, providing relief from the discomfort associated with acne.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Yarrow tea's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce redness and swelling associated with acne, as it contains flavonoids and sesquiterpene lactones that have anti-inflammatory effects.
Antimicrobial: Yarrow tea's antimicrobial properties help combat acne-causing bacteria, as it contains alkaloids, lactones, and tannins that inhibit bacterial growth.
Astringent: Yarrow tea's astringent properties help dry out excess oil and tighten pores, as it contains tannins that constrict skin tissue.
Wound-healing: Yarrow tea's wound-healing properties help speed up the healing process of acne lesions, as it contains flavonoids, alkaloids, and lactones that promote tissue regeneration.
Analgesic: Yarrow tea's analgesic properties help alleviate pain and discomfort associated with acne, as it contains sesquiterpene lactones and alkaloids that have pain-relieving effects.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Contain flavonoids and terpene compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, helping to reduce acne.
Flowers: Rich in azulene, which has anti-inflammatory properties, making it beneficial for soothing acne-prone skin.
Stems: Contain similar medicinal compounds as leaves, contributing to the plant's overall effectiveness in treating acne.
Seeds: Have high levels of essential fatty acids and antioxidants, promoting skin health and reducing acne inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Measure 1-2 teaspoons of dried yarrow flowers and leaves.
Place the herbs in a tea infuser or teapot with a strainer.
Pour 8 ounces of boiling water over the yarrow herbs.
Steep the tea for 5-10 minutes, then remove the infuser or strain the tea.
Drink the warm yarrow tea up to three times a day for acne relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold tea helps with acne because it has anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that can reduce redness and swelling, while also fighting the bacteria that can cause acne. The tea is made from the flowers of the pot marigold plant, which contain flavonoids and carotenoids that have been shown to have a calming effect on the skin, helping to soothe irritation and reduce the appearance of acne. Additionally, pot marigold tea is rich in antioxidants that can help protect the skin from damage and promote healing, making it a great choice for those looking for a natural way to improve their skin health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Pot marigold tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and carotenoids, which help reduce redness and swelling associated with acne.
Antibacterial: The plant has antibacterial properties due to its active constituents, such as essential oils and alkaloids, that help combat the bacteria causing acne.
Wound-healing: Pot marigold tea contains tannins and saponins, which promote wound healing, helping to reduce the appearance of acne scars.
Antioxidant: The tea's high antioxidant content, from flavonoids and carotenoids, helps protect skin cells from damage, reducing the likelihood of acne formation.
Astringent: Pot marigold tea has astringent properties due to its tannin content, helping to tighten pores and prevent the buildup of sebum, reducing the risk of acne.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Pot marigold flowers are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, making them ideal for soothing acne-prone skin.
Leaves: The leaves of the pot marigold plant contain flavonoids and carotenoids, which help reduce redness and irritation caused by acne.
Seeds: Pot marigold seeds are a great source of essential fatty acids and vitamin E, promoting skin health and helping to alleviate acne symptoms.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, combine 2 cups of water and 2 tablespoons of dried marigold flowers.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce heat and let it simmer for 10 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the marigold flowers, leaving only the liquid.
Allow the tea to cool down to a drinkable temperature.
Drink 1-2 cups of the marigold tea daily for best results.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: White tea helps with acne because it is rich in antioxidants, particularly EGCG, which helps reduce inflammation and prevents the growth of acne-causing bacteria. It also contains anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing redness and swelling associated with acne. Moreover, white tea has antimicrobial properties that inhibit the growth of Propionibacterium acnes, a bacterium that contributes to the development of acne. By reducing inflammation, preventing bacterial growth, and soothing irritated skin, white tea can help alleviate acne symptoms and promote clearer, healthier-looking skin.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: White tea's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce redness and swelling associated with acne, thanks to the presence of flavonoids and polyphenols.
Antibacterial property: White tea has antibacterial properties that help control the growth of acne-causing bacteria, due to the catechins, particularly epigallocatechin gallate (EGCG).
Antioxidant property: White tea's high antioxidant content protects skin cells from damage caused by free radicals, reducing the likelihood of acne formation, owing to the high levels of polyphenols.
Regulating sebum production: White tea can help regulate sebum production, preventing clogged pores that lead to acne, which is facilitated by the tea's tannins.

Skin cell rejuvenation: White tea promotes skin cell rejuvenation, aiding in the healing process of acne and reducing the appearance of scars and blemishes, due to its richness in phenolic acids, amino acids, and enzymes.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Contain high levels of antioxidants that help reduce inflammation and fight acne-causing bacteria.
Buds: Rich in polyphenols and catechins, which have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties beneficial for treating acne.
Flowers: Contain flavonoids and essential oils that help soothe skin, reduce redness, and alleviate acne symptoms.
Seeds: Packed with antioxidants and fatty acids, they can help regulate sebum production and improve overall skin health.
remedy_recipe: Boil 1 cup of water and let it cool down to 160-180°F (71-82°C), an ideal temperature for white tea.
Add 1 teaspoon of dried organic white tea leaves or 1 tea bag to a cup or teapot.
Pour the hot water over the tea leaves and let it steep for 1-3 minutes to extract the beneficial compounds.
Add 1 teaspoon of dried organic rose petals and 1-2 slices of fresh ginger root to enhance the tea's anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
Strain the tea and let it cool down before drinking or using it as a toner for acne-prone skin.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: German chamomile tea helps with acne because it has anti-inflammatory and healing properties that can reduce redness, swelling, and irritation associated with acne. It contains active compounds like alpha-bisabolol and matricin, which are converted to chamazulene when the plant is dried and heated, giving chamomile its characteristic blue color. These compounds have been shown to inhibit the growth of Propionibacterium acnes, a bacterium that contributes to the development of acne. Additionally, chamomile has antioxidant properties that can help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate acne. Drinking chamomile tea or using it as a compress can help soothe and calm irritated skin, promoting overall skin health and reducing the appearance of acne.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: German chamomile tea contains the anti-inflammatory compound chamazulene, which helps reduce inflammation and redness associated with acne.
Antimicrobial: The antimicrobial properties of German chamomile tea, due to its content of α-bisabolol and matricin, help control acne-causing bacteria.
Wound healing: German chamomile tea promotes wound healing, reducing the time needed for acne lesions to heal, thanks to its flavonoids and essential oils.
Antioxidant: The high antioxidant content, including terpenoids and flavonoids, in German chamomile tea helps protect the skin from oxidative stress and damage, which can exacerbate acne.
Soothing: German chamomile tea has soothing properties, alleviating skin irritation and providing relief for acne-prone skin, owing to its apigenin and luteolin constituents.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties, chamomile flowers help soothe skin irritation and reduce the redness associated with acne.
Leaves: Containing flavonoids and terpenoids, chamomile leaves may help decrease inflammation and provide mild antimicrobial effects, contributing to the management of acne.
remedy_recipe: Measure 1-2 teaspoons of dried German chamomile flowers and place them in a tea infuser or teapot.
Boil 8 ounces of water and pour it over the chamomile flowers.
Allow the tea to steep for 5-10 minutes, depending on desired strength.
Remove the tea infuser or strain the tea.
Drink the tea while it's still warm, up to three times a day for best results.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chicory tea helps with acne because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce redness and swelling associated with acne. It contains antioxidants that help to eliminate toxins from the body, promoting clearer skin. Chicory tea is also known to support the liver's function in eliminating waste and toxins, which can contribute to acne. Additionally, chicory tea has natural antibacterial properties that can help to kill the bacteria that cause acne. Its polyphenols and vitamin C content can help to reduce the production of sebum, which can clog pores and lead to acne. Drinking chicory tea regularly can help to maintain clear and healthy skin.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Chicory tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds such as sesquiterpene lactones, which help reduce inflammation associated with acne, making it less red and swollen.

Antioxidant: The high antioxidant content, including polyphenols and flavonoids, in chicory tea helps protect skin cells from damage caused by free radicals, promoting healthier skin and reducing the appearance of acne.
Antimicrobial: Chicory tea has antimicrobial properties due to its content of lactucin and lactucopicrin, which help control the growth of acne-causing bacteria, preventing further breakouts.

Blood sugar regulation: Chicory tea contains inulin, a soluble fiber that helps regulate blood sugar levels. This is important for acne as high blood sugar can increase sebum production, leading to clogged pores and acne.

Detoxification: Chicory tea supports the liver and promotes detoxification, aiding in the removal of toxins from the body. This can help reduce the overall toxic load on the skin, reducing the likelihood of acne breakouts.
remedy_parts: Roots: Chicory tea made from roots is known to have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce acne.
Leaves: Rich in antioxidants, chicory tea prepared from leaves can help in reducing the production of sebum, thereby controlling acne.
Flowers: Chicory tea made from flowers has antimicrobial properties that can help in treating acne and reducing the risk of skin infections.
Seeds: Chicory tea prepared from seeds can help in detoxifying the body, which can help in reducing acne and improving overall skin health.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, boil 2 cups of water and add 2 tablespoons of dried chicory root.
Reduce heat and let it simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup, discarding the solids.
Add honey or lemon to taste, if desired.
Drink the tea while warm, 1-2 times a day, for best results.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Eastern purple coneflower tea helps with acne because it contains high levels of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties. These properties work to reduce redness and swelling of the skin, which are common symptoms of acne. Additionally, the tea has antibacterial properties that help to eliminate the bacteria that cause acne. The tea also helps to regulate sebum production, which can clog pores and lead to acne. By drinking eastern purple coneflower tea regularly, individuals can improve their skin's overall health and reduce the occurrence of acne.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Eastern purple coneflower tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds, such as cichoric acid and chlorogenic acid, which help reduce redness and swelling associated with acne.
Antimicrobial: The tea has antimicrobial properties due to the presence of phenolic acids, which inhibit the growth of acne-causing bacteria.
Antioxidant: Eastern purple coneflower tea is rich in antioxidants, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, that help protect the skin from oxidative damage and reduce the development of acne.
Wound healing: The tea promotes wound healing and tissue regeneration, thanks to its high content of tannins and flavonoids, which can help speed up the recovery process of acne-related skin damage.

Regulating sebum production: Eastern purple coneflower tea has been shown to have a sebum-regulating effect, which can help balance oil production in the skin and reduce the likelihood of acne breakouts. This is attributed to the presence of active compounds like cichoric acid and chlorogenic acid.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in antioxidants, the flowers help reduce inflammation and fight acne-causing bacteria.
Leaves: Contain high levels of tannins and volatile oils, which have astringent and antibacterial properties beneficial for treating acne.
Buds: High in antioxidants and antibacterial compounds, buds can help soothe irritated skin and prevent acne breakouts.
Seeds: Seeds are a great source of essential fatty acids, promoting skin health and reducing inflammation that contributes to acne.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, combine 2 cups of water and 2 tablespoons of dried Eastern purple coneflower (Echinacea purpurea) flowers and leaves.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce heat and let it simmer for 15 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the plant material, then let it cool to a drinkable temperature.
Add honey or lemon to taste, if desired.
Drink 2-3 cups of this tea daily, consistently, for best results in managing acne.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Horsetail tea helps with acne because it is rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, such as silica, which can reduce redness and swelling of the skin. Additionally, horsetail tea has antibacterial properties that can help eliminate acne-causing bacteria, while its astringent properties can help tighten pores and reduce oil production. Furthermore, horsetail tea is high in minerals like zinc and selenium, which are known to support skin health and reduce inflammation. Drinking horsetail tea regularly can help promote clear, healthy skin and reduce the frequency and severity of acne breakouts.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Horsetail tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help reduce redness and swelling associated with acne.
Antimicrobial: Horsetail tea has antimicrobial properties due to the presence of phenolic acids, which inhibit the growth of acne-causing bacteria.
Astringent: The astringent properties of horsetail tea, attributed to tannins, help dry out excess oil and tighten pores, reducing the likelihood of acne formation.
Antioxidant: Horsetail tea contains antioxidants, including silica and flavonoids, that protect skin cells from damage caused by free radicals, promoting skin health and reducing acne.
Diuretic: Horsetail tea has diuretic properties, mainly due to the presence of equisetin, which helps eliminate toxins and reduce fluid retention, supporting overall skin health and acne prevention.
remedy_parts: Stems: The stems of the horsetail tea plant are used in medicinal tea for acne due to their high silica content, which can help to improve skin health and reduce inflammation.
Leaves: The leaves of the horsetail tea plant are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, making them useful for treating acne and promoting skin healing.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of the horsetail tea plant contain a variety of beneficial compounds, including saponins and flavonoids, which can help to reduce redness and irritation associated with acne.
remedy_recipe: Boil 1 cup of water and let it cool down for a few minutes.
Add 1-2 teaspoons of dried horsetail herb to a tea infuser or teapot.
Pour the hot water over the horsetail herb and let it steep for 5-10 minutes.
Remove the horsetail herb from the tea and let it cool down to a drinkable temperature.
Drink 1-2 cups of horsetail tea daily for best results in reducing acne.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel tea helps with acne because it has anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that can reduce redness and swelling, and help to fight the bacteria that can cause acne. It also has high levels of antioxidants that can help to protect the skin from damage and promote healing. Additionally, fennel tea can help to regulate hormonal imbalances that can contribute to acne. The tea's natural detoxifying properties can help to eliminate toxins from the body, which can also help to improve the overall health and appearance of the skin. Drinking fennel tea regularly can be a natural and effective way to help manage and treat acne.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Fennel tea's anti-inflammatory properties reduce redness and swelling, which helps alleviate acne by calming irritated skin. This is due to the presence of active constituents like flavonoids and phenolic acids.

Antibacterial: Fennel tea's antibacterial properties inhibit the growth of acne-causing bacteria, preventing further breakouts and infections on the skin. The presence of essential oils like trans-anethole and fenchone contribute to these antibacterial effects.

Antioxidant: Fennel tea's high antioxidant content helps protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, reducing the appearance of acne and promoting overall skin health. The antioxidant properties come from flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other polyphenols found in fennel.

Anti-androgenic: Fennel tea has anti-androgenic properties, which can help regulate sebum production and reduce the occurrence of acne. This is due to the presence of active compounds like dianethole, photoanethole, and anethole.

Soothing: Fennel tea has a soothing effect on the skin, reducing irritation and providing relief from acne symptoms. This is attributed to the tea's content of volatile oils, flavonoids, and other bioactive compounds that have calming and anti-inflammatory effects.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Fennel seeds are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce acne and soothe irritated skin.
Leaves: Fennel leaves contain flavonoids and phenolic compounds that have antimicrobial properties, which can help control acne-causing bacteria.
Flowers: Fennel flowers are a good source of essential oils, which have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making them useful for treating acne.
Rhyzomes: Fennel rhyzomes have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce redness, swelling, and irritation caused by acne.
remedy_recipe: Boil one and a half cups of water in a pot.
Add one teaspoon of crushed fennel seeds to the boiling water.
Reduce heat and let the mixture simmer for 5-10 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the fennel seeds.
Allow the tea to cool down, then drink it while it's still warm.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice tea helps with acne because it contains a compound called glabridin, which has anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. These properties can help reduce redness and swelling associated with acne, as well as inhibit the growth of bacteria that can cause breakouts. Additionally, licorice tea may help regulate the production of sebum, an oily substance that can clog pores and lead to acne. By reducing sebum production, licorice tea can help prevent the development of new acne lesions. Furthermore, the antioxidants found in licorice tea may help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to the development of acne. Drinking licorice tea regularly may provide a natural and effective way to manage acne symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Licorice tea contains compounds such as glycyrrhizin and flavonoids that help reduce inflammation, which is important for acne as it can help alleviate redness, swelling, and pain associated with acne lesions.

Antimicrobial: The antimicrobial properties of licorice tea, due to the presence of compounds like glycyrrhizic acid and flavonoids, can help inhibit the growth of Propionibacterium acnes, a bacterium that contributes to acne development.

Anti-androgenic: Licorice tea has anti-androgenic effects, which means it can help regulate sebum production and reduce the risk of clogged pores. This is important for acne as excessive sebum production can lead to acne lesions.

Antioxidant: The high antioxidant content in licorice tea, provided by flavonoids and other polyphenols, helps protect skin cells from oxidative damage and inflammation, which can contribute to acne development.

Soothing: Licorice tea has soothing properties, which can help reduce skin irritation and promote skin healing. This is important for acne as it can help alleviate the discomfort and appearance of acne lesions.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice tea plant contain compounds that can reduce inflammation and help soothe skin irritation, making them effective in treating acne.
Leaves: The leaves of the licorice tea plant have antimicrobial properties that can help reduce the growth of acne-causing bacteria.
Flowers: The flowers of the licorice tea plant contain flavonoids that have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce redness and swelling associated with acne.
Seeds: The seeds of the licorice tea plant are rich in antioxidants that can help protect the skin from damage and promote healing, making them useful in treating acne.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, boil 2 cups of water and add 2 tablespoons of dried licorice root, simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Turn off heat, cover and let the tea steep for an additional 10 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove licorice root pieces.
Add honey or lemon to taste, if desired.
Drink the tea while warm, up to three times a day for best results.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sea buckthorn tea helps with acne because it is rich in antioxidants, vitamins, and minerals that can support skin health. This tea is particularly high in vitamin C, which is known to promote collagen production and help repair damaged skin. Additionally, sea buckthorn tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds that can reduce redness and swelling associated with acne. The tea may also help regulate sebum production, which can prevent the clogged pores that lead to breakouts. Drinking sea buckthorn tea regularly can provide a natural and gentle way to support clear and healthy skin.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Sea buckthorn tea reduces inflammation, which is important for acne as it helps to alleviate redness, swelling, and pain associated with acne lesions. This property is attributed to the presence of flavonoids and other polyphenolic compounds.

Antioxidant: The tea has antioxidant properties that help protect skin cells from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to acne development. Active constituents responsible for this property include vitamins C and E, as well as carotenoids.

Antibacterial: Sea buckthorn tea exhibits antibacterial properties, which are crucial for acne treatment as they help to control the growth of Propionibacterium acnes, a bacterium that contributes to acne formation. The antibacterial activity can be attributed to the presence of various compounds, such as flavonoids, tannins, and essential oils.

Wound healing: The tea promotes wound healing, which is important for acne as it aids in the recovery of damaged skin tissue and minimizes the appearance of acne scars. This property is due to the presence of various compounds, including vitamins C and E, carotenoids, and flavonoids.

Sebum regulation: Sea buckthorn tea helps regulate sebum production, which is important for acne as excessive sebum can clog pores and create an environment conducive to acne-causing bacteria. This property can be attributed to the presence of unsaturated fatty acids, such as palmitoleic and linoleic acids.
remedy_parts: Berries: Sea buckthorn berries are rich in antioxidants, vitamins, and essential fatty acids, making them effective in reducing inflammation and promoting skin healing, which can help alleviate acne.
Leaves: Sea buckthorn leaves contain flavonoids and other phytochemicals that have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which can help reduce redness and swelling associated with acne.

Seeds: Sea buckthorn seeds are a great source of essential fatty acids, particularly omega-7, which has been shown to improve skin health, reduce inflammation, and promote skin regeneration, all of which can help manage acne.
remedy_recipe: Boil 1 cup of water and add 1 teaspoon of dried sea buckthorn leaves, letting it steep for 5-10 minutes.
While the tea is steeping, finely chop 1 tablespoon of fresh or dried sea buckthorn berries.
After the steeping time, strain the tea into a cup and discard the leaves.
Add the chopped sea buckthorn berries to the hot tea and let it steep for an additional 2-3 minutes.
Stir well, strain out the berries, and enjoy the herbal sea buckthorn tea while it's still warm.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat acne with herbal teas is by regularly drinking chamomile tea, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce redness and swelling. Another effective herbal tea is green tea, rich in antioxidants that combat acne-causing bacteria and reduce excess oil production. Additionally, spearmint tea can help regulate hormonal imbalances that often trigger acne. Regularly consuming these herbal teas, along with a balanced diet and skincare routine, can significantly improve acne-prone skin.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to acne that are treated with herbal teas include eczema, dermatitis, and psoriasis. Chamomile tea is known for its anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe irritated skin and reduce redness. Burdock root tea is often used as a blood purifier, helping to eliminate toxins from the body that can cause skin issues. Nettle leaf tea has antihistamine properties that can help reduce inflammation and itching. Lastly, calendula tea is known for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects, making it a great option for treating skin irritations and infections. However, it is important to consult with a healthcare provider before starting any new herbal remedies.
